private.name: NODA AI

data.narrative:
Smack Technologies is a Series A defense and security company founded by Andy Markoff (ex-MARSOC, Palantir) and Clint Alanis (20+ years Marine special ops), which has raised $32M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private-market peers in Defense & Security at the same or adjacent stage, supplemented by 2 public comps, including a peer median valuation of $1.0B across the cohort (range: $125M–$3.0B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to $32M raised as a proxy and applying a standard 2.0x Series A step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $64M. Peer-median analysis across the 8-company comparable set yields $1.0B. The sample includes Dream Security ($3.0B Series B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B Series B), Code Metal ($1.2B Series B), and Castelion and Defense Unicorns (each $1.0B Series B).

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $54M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $532M. The substantial dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty around whether Smack's technology and founder pedigree command a premium relative to earlier-stage cohort peers or will track closer to the base-case stage progression. Key uncertainties include product-market traction, customer concentration, and regulatory pathway clarity within the defense sector.
